title: CI: GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with all 8 quality gates
description:
## Gap
COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC lines 296-317 define 8 fail-closed CI gates (G1-G8) but NO automated pipeline exists. Gates exist only as local scripts. The spec requires 'strict G1→G2→...→G8 with short-circuit semantics' but there is no GitHub Actions workflow enforcing this.

## The 8 Gates (from spec)
G1: cargo fmt --check (formatting)
G2: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (lints)
G3: cargo test --workspace (Rust unit+integration tests)
G4: pytest tests/python/ -v (Python parity tests)
G5: Conformance gate (fnx-conformance fixtures vs oracle)
G6: Performance budget gate (p50/p95/p99 within SLO)
G7: UBS scan (ultimate bug scanner)
G8: RaptorQ sidecar integrity scrub

## Implementation plan

### .github/workflows/ci.yml (on push + PR)
- Trigger: push to main, pull_request to main
- Matrix: ubuntu-latest (primary), optional macOS/Windows smoke
- Jobs (sequential, fail-fast):
  1. fmt-check: cargo fmt --check
  2. clippy: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
  3. rust-tests: cargo test --workspace
  4. python-tests: maturin develop + pytest tests/python/ -v --tb=long
  5. conformance: cargo test -p fnx-conformance (all fixture gates)
  6. perf-check: run scripts/run_benchmark_gate.sh (if budget data exists)

### .github/workflows/release.yml (on tag push — see PyPI bead)

### Branch protection
- Require CI to pass before merging to main
- Require at least G1-G5 to pass

## Testing requirements
- Push a test commit, verify CI runs
- Intentionally break formatting → CI must fail at G1
- Intentionally break a test → CI must fail at G3/G4
- Verify short-circuit: if G1 fails, G2-G8 don't run

## Files to create
- .github/workflows/ci.yml
- .github/workflows/release.yml (shared with PyPI bead)

## Acceptance criteria
- Every push to main triggers CI
- Every PR triggers CI
- Failure at any gate blocks merge
- Gates run in order with short-circuit
- CI completes in < 15 minutes

## Why this matters
Without CI, quality gates are honor-system only. Multiple agents work concurrently and can introduce regressions. The spec REQUIRES automated enforcement.
close_reason: Completed

title: [CONFORMANCE] Wide parity matrix harness slice 1
description: Add a table-driven fnx-vs-upstream-networkx differential harness covering dozens of public algorithms across empty, singleton, undirected, weighted, directed, disconnected, and multigraph fixtures. Expected divergences are xfailed and tied to follow-up review beads.
close_reason: Landed tests/python/test_wide_parity_matrix.py with 814 wide matrix checks: 758 passing and 56 expected divergences tracked by review beads.

title: [HIGH] REVIEW: traversal empty/singleton contract divergences from wide matrix
description: The wide parity matrix found traversal contract gaps: bfs_edges, dfs_edges, bfs_predecessors, bfs_successors, dfs_tree, dfs_preorder_nodes, and dfs_postorder_nodes raise NodeNotFound on empty graphs where upstream NetworkX raises NetworkXError. bfs_successors on a singleton graph returns an empty sequence while upstream yields (source, []).
close_reason: Completed: public traversal wrappers now translate missing-source NodeNotFound into NetworkXError and bfs_successors yields (source, []) for source nodes without BFS children. Wide parity matrix now passes these cells.

title: [HIGH] REVIEW: link prediction missing-node and MultiGraph contracts diverge
description: The wide parity matrix found link-prediction gaps in jaccard_coefficient, adamic_adar_index, resource_allocation_index, and preferential_attachment. On empty-graph ebunch pairs fnx returns scores where upstream raises NodeNotFound. On MultiGraph inputs fnx returns scores where upstream raises NetworkXNotImplemented.
close_reason: Completed: link-prediction PyO3 wrappers now reject explicit ebunch pairs containing missing nodes with NodeNotFound and reject all multigraph inputs with NetworkXNotImplemented before scoring. Wide parity matrix now passes these cells.

title: [HIGH] REVIEW: is_connected_dominating_set graph-family contracts diverge
description: The wide parity matrix found is_connected_dominating_set returns booleans for graph families where upstream rejects the call: null graph should raise NetworkXPointlessConcept and directed graphs should raise NetworkXNotImplemented.
close_reason: Completed: is_connected_dominating_set now raises NetworkXPointlessConcept on null graphs and NetworkXNotImplemented on directed graph families before checking domination. Wide parity matrix now passes these cells.

title: [HIGH] REVIEW: ancestors and descendants reject undirected graphs unlike NetworkX
description: The wide parity matrix found fnx.ancestors and fnx.descendants raise NetworkXError on undirected Graph and MultiGraph fixtures while upstream NetworkX returns reachable-node sets using the graph's adjacency semantics.
close_reason: Completed: ancestors and descendants now support undirected graph families by returning the reachable component minus the source, and missing-node errors now match NetworkX graph/digraph wording. Wide parity matrix now passes these cells.

title: [HIGH] REVIEW: pagerank weighted and MultiGraph parity gaps
description: The wide parity matrix found pagerank value divergences from upstream NetworkX on weighted undirected graphs and MultiGraph inputs. Root-cause likely sits in weight projection and parallel-edge aggregation semantics.
close_reason: Completed: pagerank now honors signed edge weights for simple graphs and sums parallel-edge weights for MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph projections before running the native PageRank iteration. Wide parity matrix now passes the weighted and multigraph pagerank cells.

title: [HIGH] REVIEW: unweighted shortest-path wrappers reject directed graphs
description: The wide parity matrix found single_source_shortest_path, single_source_shortest_path_length, all_pairs_shortest_path, and all_pairs_shortest_path_length raise NetworkXNotImplemented on directed graph fixtures where upstream NetworkX returns directed shortest-path results.
close_reason: Completed: unweighted single-source and all-pairs shortest-path wrappers now dispatch directed graphs through directed BFS helpers instead of rejecting them. Wide parity matrix now passes the directed shortest-path cells.

title: [HIGH] REVIEW: directed component and topological error contracts diverge
description: The wide parity matrix found strongly_connected_components yields SCCs in a different order from upstream NetworkX on directed fixtures. It also found topological_sort on a cyclic digraph raises HasACycle where upstream raises NetworkXUnfeasible.
close_reason: Fixed: SCC emits in nx sink-first order; topological_sort/generations/lexicographic raise NetworkXUnfeasible (not HasACycle) on cyclic input.

title: [HIGH] REVIEW: local_bridges graph-family rejection contracts diverge
description: The wide parity matrix found local_bridges returns results on directed graphs and MultiGraph inputs where upstream NetworkX raises NetworkXNotImplemented.
close_reason: Fixed: local_bridges raises NetworkXNotImplemented on directed and multigraph inputs, matching @not_implemented_for decorator.

title: [HIGH] REVIEW: MultiGraph structural algorithms diverge from NetworkX
description: The wide parity matrix found MultiGraph-specific structural gaps: is_tree and is_forest return true despite parallel edges where upstream returns false; bridges includes the parallel edge pair that upstream excludes; degree_centrality undercounts parallel-edge degree; complement raises TypeError where upstream returns a MultiGraph complement.
close_reason: Fixed in cycle-continuation commit: Python overrides for is_tree/is_forest/bridges/degree_centrality/complement on MultiGraph input match nx contract (count parallels, exclude parallel-edge bridges, delegate complement to nx for MG).
